consequences: The post stays focused on first-order constraint diagnosis rather than funding-market microstructure. The rollover and external-constraint additions cover the omitted factors that most directly alter the main argument. Holder composition remains the highest-value remaining extension, and a future repo/liquidity model is justified only for a later post centered on crisis transmission, dealer balance sheets, haircuts, or safe-collateral shortages.
context: The factor-refinement cycle added sovereign_securities.py for maturity/rollover dynamics and external_constraint.py for import dependence and FX stress. With those higher-value qualifiers now modeled, the remaining question is whether this post also needs a dedicated repo/collateral/liquidity model.
decision: Do not build a repo/collateral/liquidity model in this cycle. Keep repo/collateral as explicit limitation language and operational context in the prose.
title: Keep repo/collateral as a scoped limitation in this cycle
